messages.content:
I am writing a greasemonkey(or tampermonkey) script for a page that you type in a prompt, click generate, it shows a progress bar then the image pops up, or an error message pops up, the entire page does not refresh(under normal circumstances)

How could I potentially get any of the loading "events" on the page? For now I just want to get everything, and print to the console any actual load events, logging them in such a way to be minimal and if a particular event happens too quickly(more than once a second) it will not log it again during that one second.
And finally, I want to be able to know if the entire page refreshes, I assume the greasemonkey script might reload, or "init", but sometimes the website redirects, logging me out of my account, I want to be able to detect and log that as well, not just when I hit F5 to refresh the page myself.

This is good enough for a starting point, and yeah I know it logging me out will be trickier, however, it usually takes me to a page that doesn't have my greasemonkey script set to, so it is more about detecting the page loading.
One final thing actually that should complete me for the moment, if there is an error message, this XPath should exist(it will not exist if there is no error):
//*[@id="root"]/div/div/div/div[3]/div[3]/div[1]/div/div/span[2]

This span might contain either child spans with text inside those, or might be just text inside this span, I want to be able to check all text under this span, where I do it doesn't matter right now(it might be later based on events once I process them and find the ones I want to handle), but I basically want to check the text of this message and return it from a message, return null if there is no error(so I can do an if (message) { and ignore it if it returned no message)
This will let me decide how I want to do the rest of the script, so for now I just want to get the text in that span, so I just want a function that can do this for me.

There is a <textarea> node, that holds the prompt text, inside text()(so like this: <textarea>prompt</textarea>), is there an event I can tap into that triggers when this text is changed? the XPath is: //*[@id="root"]/div/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/div/div/form/div[1]/span/textarea/text()

But the span we query, does not exist if there is no error message, 
This exists when we have an image but no error message: //*[@id="root"]/div/div/div/div[3]/div[3]/div/div/button
This is the image XPath when we have an image AND the error message(this happens if we get an error after we got an image, the last image we got remains): //*[@id="root"]/div/div/div/div[3]/div[3]/div[2]/div/button
And this is the error message when we have one, regardless of if there is an image or not: //*[@id="root"]/div/div/div/div[3]/div[3]/div[1]/div/div/span[2]

As you can see, the error message gets inserted into the page when it happens, if we get the next image with no errors, the message goes away in the same way.

This is why if we try to document.evaluate on that span with no error message, it throws an error.

How careless of me, the UI I am using to communicate with you turns all your outputs to use those special characters, and erase *, so when I copy the code you give, I have to go any manually to correct those symbols, it appears this time I forgot to do this, and it caused me this issue, so my mistake.

It works now, thank you!

Just one final thing though, I want to match on the error message returned, using a fuzzy "contains" match method, like, does errorMessage contain "this text".
I will want a series of options.

No, what I mean by options is, if thisText is in the error message, do this, if thatText is in the error message, do that, else, do a default action(which I guess is to do the standard procedure, since there was no error)

They should be numbers, however I can't just "let" them like you gave:

`async function startSequence(count, max, retry) {`
They are both passed in to the function
